title: Elements from FLCL Present in 20
wikitext:
[]: Elements from FLCL Present in 2.0             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Mar 05, 2011 1:30 pm Mecha gains enormous power, turns red. Half of an areola visible only for an instant, "concealed" by frenetic bouncing The climax of "Full Swing" seems to be a direct descendant of Neon Genesis Evangelion 's Sahaquiel fight. The "transformation" element of the hurtling object changing form as pieces break off was in FLCL first, then was reappropriated for 2.0 . EDIT: Ah, right! The ending! SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Sat Mar 05, 2011 1:39 pm Nice observations! There's also that vortex that starts sucking everything up (although it only sucks up the Pheonix if I recall correctly in FLCL as opposed to a lot of ground n stuff).

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Mar 05, 2011 1:40 pm It sucks up the Medical Mechanica hand in a swirly fashion, yes.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 05, 2011 4:04 pm You should add that stuff to here, NAW-kun~! http://wiki.evageeks.org/Gainax:_Evangelion_References_and_Cross_References

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Mar 05, 2011 5:59 pm Reichu wrote: You should add that stuff to here, NAW-kun~! http://wiki.evageeks.org/Gainax:_Evangelion_References_and_Cross_References

Article: Elements from FLCL Present in 20
[]: Elements from FLCL Present in 2.0             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Mar 05, 2011 1:30 pm Mecha gains enormous power, turns red. Half of an areola visible only for an instant, "concealed" by frenetic bouncing The climax of "Full Swing" seems to be a direct descendant of Neon Genesis Evangelion 's Sahaquiel fight. The "transformation" element of the hurtling object changing form as pieces break off was in FLCL first, then was reappropriated for 2.0 . EDIT: Ah, right! The ending! SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Sat Mar 05, 2011 1:39 pm Nice observations! There's also that vortex that starts sucking everything up (although it only sucks up the Pheonix if I recall correctly in FLCL as opposed to a lot of ground n stuff).

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Mar 05, 2011 1:40 pm It sucks up the Medical Mechanica hand in a swirly fashion, yes.
